title: polygonum aviculare subsp. buxiforme
intro: Polygonum aviculare subsp. buxiforme, also known as knotweed or knotgrass, is a subspecies of the Polygonum aviculare plant, a flowering plant in the buckwheat family (Polygonaceae). It has been traditionally used in herbal medicine for its medicinal properties, including its antiseptic, anti-inflammatory, and antioxidant effects. The health benefits of Polygonum aviculare subsp. buxiforme include reducing inflammation, improving wound healing, and alleviating symptoms of respiratory infections. The plant can be prepared in various herbal preparations, such as infusions, decoctions, and tinctures, to be consumed as a tea or taken internally to harness its medicinal properties.
